title: Commentary on a method for testing resistance to shocks
author: Narayan

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: The Narayan [26] resistance methodology is in turn based on the Narayan and Popp [23], see also Narayan and Popp [22], unit root (NP-UR) test.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 1: Implement the NP-UR model that allows for two endogenous breaks in the level. There are no breaks in the trend.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 2: Choose an appropriate trimming factor to account for tails of the data. Typically, a 10%-20% trimming factor is employed depending on the sample size.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 3: Choose the initial data sample window to generate time-varying β. Narayan (2020) recommend a window size of 20%. Alternative window sizes may be motivated based on sample size.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 4: Run the following regression model using ordinary least squares:(1)OILt=α+βOILt−1+λt+κ1BRB,1′+κ2BRB,2′+δ1DU1,t−1′+δ2DU2,t−1′+∑j=1kπjΔOILt−j+et

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: The variable which we consider for the test of resistance is the WTI crude oil price (OIL).

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 5: Choose the lag length, k, which should be set high enough to accommodate serial correlation. When done, use one of the lag length selection criteria to select the optimal lag length.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 6: Obtain the BRB,1and BRB,2 which denote, respectively, the first and second break dates; and DU1 and DU2 are the level break dummy variables.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: 0Step 7: Test the null hypothesis of a unit root by setting β=1 against the alternative hypothesis that β<1.

steps.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps.instruction: Step 8: Use the critical values from NP [23] to decide on the rejection or otherwise of the unit root null hypothesis.
